title: Mocha CFW
requirements: A Wii U on the latest firmware (5.5.6 or lower in North America, 5.5.5 or lower elsewhere)
An SD card formatted as FAT32 (a microSD with an adapter is fine)
The Wii U connected to the internet
warnings: Mocha is a legacy method. Aroma is the recommended way to hack a Wii U today.
Every time you modify a Wii U there is a !!small chance of an unrecoverable brick!!. Follow each step exactly.
Mocha CFW is temporary and is not compatible with the Homebrew Launcher Channel.
Make a NAND backup when the guide tells you to. Don't skip it.
_note: Legacy Mocha method (online-exploit entrypoint, with optional Indexiine for offline persistence), transcribed from the old homebrew-guide (docsify) Wii U guide. Downloads come live from the linked GitHub releases and the fortheusers CDN.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body:
**Mocha** is the free Wii U CFW that needs no purchased game. It runs through the browser exploit and is **temporary**: you re-launch it after every reboot.

This guide uses the **Online Exploit** entrypoint (needs internet each launch). You can optionally add **Indexiine** at the end so the Homebrew Launcher triggers offline from the browser.

Modern hacking uses **Aroma**, so only use Mocha if you have a specific reason. Make sure your Wii U is on the **latest firmware** and your SD card is **FAT32** (verified below). Never name the SD card `wiiu`.

steps.title: Put the files on your SD card
steps.body:
Graphite downloads the latest homebrew and unpacks each piece into the right folder on your SD card:

- The **Homebrew Launcher** and its installer payload
- **WUP Installer GX2**, the **Wii U NAND Dumper**, and the **Homebrew App Store** installer
- **Mocha CFW**
- The **Indexiine Installer** (for the optional offline-launch step later)

This is everything the guide would normally have you download by hand. Just press the button and wait.

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS quietly scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards, and Wii U homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull the card out.

steps.title: Run the browser exploit
steps.body:
Put the SD card into the **Wii U** and make sure it's online.

Open the **Internet Browser** and go to `wiiuexploit.xyz`. Tap **Run Homebrew Launcher!** The console should boot into the **Homebrew Launcher**.

If the screen freezes or goes white, wait a few seconds; if nothing happens, reboot, clear the browser's saved data, and try again.

troubleshoot.label: I've got an error on the browser

steps.title: Make a NAND backup
steps.body:
!!Your safety net!!. In the Homebrew Launcher, open **Wii U NAND Dumper**.

Set it up like the picture: **SLC: Yes, SLCCMPT: Yes, OTP: Yes, SEEPROM: Yes**. Leave **MLC** on **No** unless your SD card is bigger than the console's storage.

Press **A** to dump. When it finishes, power off the Wii U.

steps.title: Save your NAND backup
steps.body:
Take the SD card out of the Wii U and plug it back into this computer.

Press the button and Graphite grabs the backup the console just made (`slc.bin`, `otp.bin`, and the rest), copies it into a folder on your **Desktop**, and clears the files off the SD card to free up space. If a backup folder already exists, it saves a new one alongside it.

This is optional; you can skip ahead with **Next**, but keeping the backup is strongly recommended.

steps.title: Launch Mocha CFW
steps.body:
Put the card back in the Wii U and run the browser exploit to reach the Homebrew Launcher.

1. Open the **Mocha CFW** app.
2. It returns you to the Homebrew Launcher with **CFW enabled**.

That's it, but CFW is temporary. You must **re-do these steps every reboot** to re-enable Mocha. To skip re-visiting the exploit URL each time, add Indexiine in the next (optional) step.

steps.title: Install Indexiine for offline launch (optional)
steps.body:
Indexiine modifies the browser's saved home page so the Homebrew Launcher triggers **without an internet connection**. It requires editing a system file, so it's optional.

1. Launch the Homebrew Launcher via the browser exploit (as above).
2. Open the **Indexiine Installer** app and press **A** to install.
3. When done, close the Homebrew Launcher to return to the Wii U Menu.

Now the Homebrew Launcher launches every time you open the Internet Browser, even offline. (To visit a real website, open it *before* Indexiine triggers.) You still open **Mocha CFW** each boot to enable CFW.

troubleshoot.label: I've got an error on the browser

steps.title: You're done
steps.body:
Mocha CFW is set up. Launch the Homebrew Launcher, then the **Mocha CFW** app, each time you boot to enable CFW. Drop homebrew into `wiiu/apps` on the SD card.

Note: Mocha is not compatible with the Homebrew Launcher Channel. If you want automatic boot-time CFW, look at **CBHC** instead (requires a paid DS game).
